reviews.review_description: It's a short 10 minute story with toy environments. The toy environments look nice but have mediocre interactions. The story is nothing special, it seems? (I wasn't paying much attention)
Everything about this is beautiful. Capture the magic of your childhood and relax and meditate with this wonderful adventure. Everything is Beautiful here. Thank you

reviews.review_title: A bit hit and miss but enjoyed it
reviews.review_description:
The hand tracking could be a bit janky at times but that may more be metas fault , I don’t use hand tracking often but when I do it’s usually been jinxed by an update.
The atmosphere / story / sounds were all good ,
Visually some parts were a bit too dark but overall were good.
The activities were varied. The hand tracking again worked better on some more than others.
It only took about 10 minutes to play through but you can replay it - it’s quite a relaxing experience
I can’t think of anything really bad about it so I’ll give it 5 stars. I enjoyed it, it didn’t cost much and it’s good to try different things.
Give it a bit more polish and hopefully you’ll get it on the main store with a wider audience.

Lovely vignettes evoke childhood nostalgia. This makes great use of hand tracking and VR's capacity for impossible locations and experiences. The visuals are gorgeous, and the performance is very smooth. Some of the interactions are a little repetitive, but overall there are a lot of moments of surprise and wonder, and it's worth going through everything. Time and money well spent!
it's super well made and is definitely worth the price personally I had a fun time in the bubble part I swear when I popped the bubble I felt it hit my arm it was amazing

Its more than a hand tracking demo. The thematic music is really nice , theres a bit of a story, and theres definitely an atmosphere about this little experience. Im not sure how some of the exercises are related to the story, and some feel janky. 

I think a few of the exercises could be removed as they are similar and the whole thing tightened up. That being said playing with the last exercise gave me goose pumps . I dont know why but that  was worth $3..

I would encourage this dev to keep going and mine the emotive aspects of VR .
Once noticing that i needed to make a tight fist to pick up objects, hand tracking felt much better🤛

The voice over in this one almost made me cry.  It is still rough around the edges, but it feels right so many times, and gives a sensation of tactility. There are many satisfying interactions in here.  I swear I could feel the pet! And popping the bubble was super awesome.  Great Job!

For a buck this is worth it. Visually beautiful at times, emotive...but the hand tracking is terrible. You can't pick up any of the little spheres. In certain scenes I had to pick up the controller to progress to the next scene.
